meaning: n.(北美印地安人的一种主要食品)干肉饼,摘要,要旨
examples: White trappers and hunters learned from the Indians how to prepare light-weight survival rations like "pemmican" and "jerked" beef.
Franklin's expedition carried nearly 8 000 lead-soldered tins containing meat, soup, vegetables and pemmican - a pressed cake of shredded dry meat.
To mark Capt Scott's legacy, Henry will take some of the rations the Terra Nova team had on their ill-fated return - which consisted of pemmican (ground meat mixed with fat) and biscuits.
